LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO LIFT DISCOVERY STAY: PLAINTIFF SUING UNDER THE FICTITIOUS NAME OF JOHN SMITH'S MOTION TO TAKE DISCOVERY TO OPPOSE DEFENDANTS' ANTI-SLAPP MOTIONS AS TO HIS SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DEFAMATION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE DISCOVERY HE PROPOSES TO TAKE WOULD ENABLE HIM TO SATISFY HIS SECOND PRONG BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THERE IS THE REQUIRED MINIMAL MERIT TO HIS DEFAMATION CLAIM. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) AN ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (D302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT YOUSEF WALLY'S ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY THE PLAINTIFF SUING UNDER THE FICTITIOUS NAME OF JOHN SMITH IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT AT THE TRIAL COURT LEVEL HIS SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MALICIOUS PROSECUTION SHOULD BE STRICKEN PER THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE. THE COURT DENIES MR. SMITH'S CLAIM TO TAKE DISCOVERY AS TO BOTH THE DEFAMATION AND ABUSE OF PROCESS CLAIMS. THE PORTIONS OF THE MOTION ON DEFAMATION AND ABUSE OF PROCESS ARE TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. BOTH SIDES MAY FILE ADDITIONAL BRIEFS BY 5:00 PM ON FRIDAY 5/11/18 SOLELY ON THE ISSUE OF WHETHER SPOILATION CAN SERVE AS ACTIONABLE ABUSE OF PROCESS. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) COUNSEL FOR MR. WALLY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (D302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS HILDA CHAN AND ASIAN PACIFIC ISLANDER LEGAL OUTREACH'S ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION ONLY. PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT AT THE TRIAL COURT LEVEL HIS SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MALICIOUS PROSECUTION SHOULD BE STRICKEN PER THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) THE COURT DENIES MR. SMITH'S CLAIM TO TAKE DISCOVERY AS TO BOTH THE DEFAMATION AND ABUSE OF PROCESS CLAIMS. THE PORTIONS OF THE MOTION ON DEFAMATION AND ABUSE OF PROCESS ARE TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. BOTH SIDES MAY FILE ADDITIONAL BRIEFS BY 5:00 PM ON FRIDAY 5/11/18 SOLELY ON THE ISSUE OF WHETHER SPOILATION CAN SERVE AS ACTIONABLE ABUSE OF PROCESS. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) COUNSEL FOR MS. CHAN IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312.JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (D302/HK)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Smith 213-270-4815 Plaintiff in Pro Per.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court informs parties he has not read the 3 volumes on case number FDV-17-813312 and shares his views. The three motion are argued simultaneously. The matter is recalled. The Court deems the submission of the 8 paged transcript as timely. Defendant opposes continuance and new evidence after reply briefs are filed. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Motion To Lift Discovery Stay, (Added To Calendar Per The Ex Parte Order Filed On 2/8/18).

The Court confirms the tentative ruling. Plaintiff suing under the fictitious name of John Smith's motion to take discovery to oppose defendants' anti-SLAPP motions as to his sixth cause of action for defamation is denied. Plaintiff has not shown that the discovery he proposes to take would enable him to satisfy his second prong burden of showing that there is the required minimal merit to his defamation claim. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Smith 213-270-4815 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Sigun Amber Lee, Esq. 415-956-2828 of Rogers Joseph O'Donell for Defendant Hilda Chan.
Tia Taruc Canlas, Esq. 510-393-2723 of Alipato Project for Defendant YOUSEF WALLY

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court informs parties he has not read the 3 volumes on case number FDV-17-813312 and shares his views. The three motion are argued simultaneously. Defendant Chan argues negligence and IIED. Plaintiff argues issue with 3 rulings, defamation by Mr. Wally. Defendant Wally argues.

11:01
The Court takes a break for Ex parte matters and orders Parties to discuss Plaintiff's request to augment the record with a transcript.

11:25 am
The matter is recalled. The Court deems the submission of the 8 paged transcript as timely. Defendant opposes continuance and new evidence after reply briefs are filed. Plantiff argues defamation as to clinic and Ms. Chan, abuse of process, spoilation of evidence. Defendants respond. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT HILDA CHAN, ASIAN PACIFIC ISLANDER LEGAL OUTREACH's MOTION TO STRIKE PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16 PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The tentative ruling is confirmed and modified. Defendants Hilda Chan and Asian Pacific Islander Legal Outreach's anti-SLAPP motion to strike is granted as to the seventh cause of action only. Plaintiff concedes that at the trial court level his seventh cause of action for malicious prosecution should be stricken per the anti-SLAPP statute. Ms. Chan and API did not satisfy their first prong burden of showing that the ninth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress arose from protected activity because the gravamen of that cause of action against Ms. Chan and API is their alleged participation in the unconsented distribution of a sensitive video that infringed on plaintiff's protected privacy rights. Ms. Chan and API did not satisfy their first prong burden of showing that the tenth cause of action for negligence arose from protected activity because that cause of action is devoid of any factual allegations. (Central Valley Hospitalists v. Dignity Health (2018) 19 Cal. App. 5th 203). As to that cause of action, moving defendants' remedy is to file a demurrer, not an anti-SLAPP motion to strike.

The Court denies Mr. Smith's claim to take discovery as to both the defamation and abuse of process claims. The portions of the motion on defamation and abuse of process are taken under submission.** Both sides may file additional briefs by 5:00 pm on Friday 5/11/18 solely on the issue of whether spoilation can serve as actionable abuse of process. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for Ms. Chan is to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

**The matter is no longer taken under submission. The matter has been reported as settled by parties on 6/16/18.**

The Court denies Mr. Smith's claim to take discovery as to both the defamation and abuse of process claims.
